rationale:
Reviewed PR #117 (head 6126645) against the frozen v15 Task 4a scope (v12 + AM-40 + AM-41), per authz-008. The deterministic gate reports finalStatus=meets with all 15 checks green (existence, weave exports, frozen-table/loader-map presence, closure derivation, check, test-all=7 tests, zero-deps, permitted imports only, diff confined to clotho/). My clause-by-clause faithfulness assessment:

(a) Closed inventory — FAITHFUL. inventory.mjs commits PACKAGE_ROOTS exactly as AM-40's five spine packages plus the explicit PACKAGE_ROOTS_EXCLUDE trio; test-inventory §7 mechanically enumerates tracked package.json dirs via a pinned no-shell `git ls-files` and proves discovered == union AND intersection == ∅, plus exact-array, sorted+unique assertions — so a new package fails until classified. DOC_ROOTS, DOC_WEAVER_EXCLUDE=["docs/runs"], GLOBAL_EXCLUDE=["docs/runs/clotho-self-weave"], CONTRACT_FILES (sorted, D31), LEDGER_SOURCES (exact-final empty with reviewed justification, not a placeholder), RUN_SOURCES, five weaver ids with integer versions, REQUIRED_INVENTORY_IDS deep-frozen and proven EXACTLY equal to the frozen normative table (test-inventory §2, including sorted/unique/frozen/mutation checks), and LOADER_CAPABLE_BUILTIN_SAFE_EXPORTS deep-frozen with deep-equal, exact keys {module,node:module}, exact sorted-pair values, deep-freeze of outer+inner, and mutation-fails-and-unchanged units (§3) — all present as written.

(b) D17/AM-17 — FAITHFUL. WEAVER_IMPL_FILES commits only git ({registry,git}) and code ({registry,code,util}) closures; WEAVER_ENTRY_MODULE covers only those two; test-inventory §4 proves every committed path exists (impl files, contract files, run sources, permitted externals). No inventory names a nonexistent file.

(c) util.mjs substrate — FAITHFUL. ONE classifier (classifyModuleLoads) + ONE resolver (resolveRelativeSpecifier) exported and consumed by BOTH deriveAcceptedClosure and code.mjs; test-closure §9 proves single definition sites across all clotho modules, that no other module appends extensions, that code.mjs imports the shared resolver by name, and that deriveAcceptedClosure's own body calls both (AM-34 test 19 satisfied for the consumers that exist at this PR; the Task 5 advisory scanner is correctly deferred). The lexer is dependency-free, comment/string/template/regex-aware with previous-significant-token regex-vs-division across expression contexts (control-head paren tracking, block-vs-object brace kinds, postfix ++/--, value-token staleness resets) — each behavior pinned by units. Phase-1 export grammar is exactly the four forms; unsupported forms (default, export*, lists, destructuring, let/var, multi-declarator) warn and emit no symbol, with property/member/method `export` occurrences correctly emitting neither. The AM-41 profile is enforced fail-closed with the stable `unsupported-module-lexical-profile` diagnostic across the exact b1–b6 set (bare CR/U+2028/U+2029; misplaced/second hashbang with the single leading shebang admitted incl. CRLF; <!-- and line-leading --> incl. whitespace-prefixed; string line-continuation and legacy octal with \0-not-digit admitted; string-literal specifier/alias names incl. export * as "ns" and no-from lists; unterminated strings/comments/templates/regex and truncated accepted forms incl. unclosed calls and unbalanced import options) — all unit-proven in test-util §14/§15/§20 with both directions (supported variation classifies correctly; out-of-profile throws ProfileError). AM-41(c) original-component containment is implemented in the SHARED resolver (originalChainSymlinkFree walks uncollapsed components before path.resolve) and fixtured with a junction+later-`..` candidate requiring rejection with kind exactly "symlink", plus the missing-component-before-symlink variant (§16). Counted iterator matches D26/D29 semantics exactly (count on completed consumption, exhaustion only on normal completion, cardinality=list length, accounting only via driver-held accessor — with a probe proving no own key of source/iterator leaks it). physicalContainment lstats every component, rejects symlink root/ancestor/intermediate/escape-target, never follows to decide — all four required fixture cases plus AM-41(c) run as junctions that must run, not skip. Git wrapper: exact allowlisted shapes only (both log shapes order-pinned; reorder/duplicate/missing/extra rejected), canonical non-option path args, execFileSync no-shell, caller options dropped and GIT_* redirect env neutralized (with a real two-repo integration proving inherited GIT_DIR cannot redirect).

(d) test-closure.mjs — FAITHFUL. Real committed inventories proven equal to derived closures through one shared harness (equality + existence + regular-file), with the committed permitted-external policy; per-form omission fixtures for every accepted form (named/side-effect/export-from/export-star incl. `* as ns` and keyword-named alias/dynamic import/require/module.require incl. whitespace member spelling); recursive mixed chain; cycle-once; unreachable-conditional dynamic import included; permitted merkle-dag helper recursively traversed; extra/missing/nonexistent inventory failures; unresolved/ambiguous/non-regular/escape/forbidden/symlink target fatality; entry subjected to the same checks; non-literal and comment/string lookalikes no-edge; direct real-repo closure equality incl. registry-leaf and inventory-leaf proofs; require-style non-.mjs targets traversed (.cjs/.json/.js) per D33 while extensionless stays ambiguous; out-of-profile entry/member/b5/b6 failing the whole closure; and allowExternal unable to widen beyond merkle-dag/.

(e) git.mjs — FAITHFUL. Exactly the two frozen log invocations as single argv tokens (-S<symbol> verbatim, metacharacter-safe, proven by exact-call assertions); first-result (earliest) selection; source_ref git:<same-sha>; every line validated as full SHA with CRLF tolerated, bare CR fatal, blank lines fatal, empty output warn+no-edge; producer-attributed warnings; own-input validation before argv splice with a spy runner proving git is never reached on bad input; closure intent pinned (imports only ../registry.mjs); byte-equal determinism over two runs; both counted sources exhausted incl. the empty-source case.

(f) code.mjs — FAITHFUL. All four depends-on endpoint shapes fixtured with exact node-id and locator-field assertions (repository_ref + blob_sha both ends, symbol on symbol-level); alias resolution; no-export named import preserved as repository-file -> code-symbol (not downgraded); namespace/side-effect/non-seeded-named threading at file level (both consumer-with-export and consumer-without-export variants); unrepresentable-consumer ONLY for unresolvable specifiers (missing .mjs, real-but-outside-roots) with non-.mjs imports silent (including missing non-.mjs — a reasonable, explicitly pinned precedence within the .mjs-only extraction grammar) and symlink/escape physical violations failing closed rather than degrading; dedup; unused imports no edge; comments/strings/regex-after-keyword not uses; counted-source exhaustion; byte-equal two-run determinism.

Scope discipline: no orchestrator inventory, no weave.mjs, no atomic publication, no D34 re-derivation, no test/doc/ledger weaver code — all correctly deferred to 4b/5. Zero runtime deps, ESM, node:+relative+permitted-vendor imports only (gate-verified), spine untouched (diff confined to clotho/).

Judgment notes (not defects): the DOC_WEAVER_EXCLUDE/GLOBAL_EXCLUDE constants are committed-but-unwired, which the plan itself schedules (doc weaver 4b, driver 5) and the code documents as deferred-not-decorative; LEDGER_SOURCES empty is a reviewed exact-final value consistent with D31's shape and is test-pinned; PERMITTED_EXTERNAL_CLOSURE_FILES lists merkle-dag/vendor.mjs though neither current closure reaches it — it is the committed forbidden-vs-permitted policy line the closure test exercises, matching the plan's 'any permitted merkle-dag primitives' clause. The b5 in-list detection relies on parseModuleDecl's brace-match string scan and the direct-clause check; unit coverage (import {"a" as b}, export {"x"} from, export {x as "public"} no-from, export * as "ns") pins all four spellings. I verified the frozen normative table character-for-character against the plan's table and the D32 mapping against the spec. This is a faithful, no-reinterpretation, no-scope-expansion implementation of Task 4a; I stake the seat's signature on approval.
